module-2-community-website-history.label: Project: Build the Community Website History
module-2-community-website-history.summary: Create a readable sequence of cohesive commits for a small community website instead of saving unrelated changes in one snapshot.

intro-sketch.title: Building a Cohesive Commit History
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown:
In this project, you'll work as a junior web maintainer for a community center website. The goal is to create a readable commit history that clearly documents changes to the website's home page and events page. This will help other developers understand the updates without needing to open every file. You'll learn to use Git commands to inspect changes, stage them selectively, and commit them with meaningful messages. By the end of this project, you'll have a clean, organized commit history that reflects the website's development accurately.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A community center website has a plain home page and an events page. Staff want the next updates recorded as a history that another developer can understand without opening every file.

**Your role:** Junior web maintainer organizing a readable local history

**Workspace:** One cumulative terminal workspace rooted at community-site/ with index.html, events.html, styles.css, and an initialized repository

**Deliverable:** A six-step history with separate navigation and events commits, exact authored messages, preserved file content, and a clean final working tree.
